key_metrics: Core net new client assets: $80.3 billion (up 31% YoY)
qoq_changes: Equity markets and investor sentiment rebounded during the second quarter following late Q1 volatility.
10-year U.S. Treasury yield ended at 4.24%, largely consistent with the end of the prior quarter.
new_risks: Uncertainty around the economic impacts of trade policy.
Increased market volatility impacting client trading dynamics.
management_tone: Bullish, as management emphasized strong asset gathering, sustained client engagement, equity market appreciation, and successful reduction of higher-cost bank supplemental funding.
analyst_note: Schwab delivered a highly profitable Q2 2025 with net income surging 60% YoY, driven by robust trading volumes, strong net interest revenue from reduced supplemental funding costs, and massive client asset growth to $10.76 trillion. The firm's ability to attract core net new assets while managing expenses positions it well for continued margin expansion.
